steps.title: Read the but perhaps shift
steps.content: Before the underlined portion, the narrator criticizes the rectory's heaviness, colors, and lighting. The phrase but perhaps then offers a possible reason for not improving those features: doing so might have damaged the rectory's clerical appearance.

steps.title: Match the explanatory turn
steps.content: Choice A misreads the portion as a concession about impressions. Choice C invents disapproval of grand furnishings for clergy. Choice D focuses only on lighting, while the underlined portion explains the broader furnishing choices. The answer is B.
